what_this_does: Converts the symmetry/skew nulls from 'failed to reject' into quantified statements: the smallest effect each test could detect (MDE) and whether the data actively bound the effect below a pre-registered negligibility threshold (TOST equivalence).
derived_not_reestimated: Point estimates and CIs are read from exceedance_paired.csv and skew_test.csv; SE is inferred from each CI half-width / z_{0.975} so MDE/TOST are exactly consistent with the published intervals.

objects.exceedance_delta: beta_down - beta_up at alpha in [0.1, 0.05, 0.01], horizon h=0 (per-period downside whisper; from exceedance_paired.csv).
objects.skew_test: beta_shock at measures ['skew_tail05', 'skew_tail01', 'z3_winsor'] (from skew_test.csv); skew_tail05/01 are tail-probability (down-up) effects [SESOI applies], z3_winsor is a third-moment response [SESOI N/A-UNITS].

formulas.mde_50_meaning: power=0.50 => z_power=0 => MDE = z_{1-alpha/2}*SE = CI half-width (smallest 'just-significant' effect).

sesoi.locked_definition: Smallest-effect-size-of-interest = a down-minus-up tail-PROBABILITY gap of 1 percentage point (0.01).
sesoi.rationale: The economic claim under test is 'no NEGLIGIBLE-or-larger downside-specific amplification'. A 1pp gap in tail-violation probability between the down and up sides is the smallest down/up asymmetry an investor/risk-manager would plausibly care about; anything smaller is economically indistinguishable from symmetry. Object betas are per-unit-shock, so the gap is mapped to beta-units via the shock's empirical span.
sesoi.zero_inflation: shock = log_liq.shift(1) is zero-inflated; in-sample p10=p25=p50=0, so IQR==p75, p50->p95==p95, p10->p90==p90. Spans reported as-is.
sesoi.span_direction_note: Smaller span => larger (more permissive) SESOI_beta; larger span => smaller (stricter) SESOI_beta. Verdicts reported under all three so the conclusion's sensitivity to the span choice is transparent.
sesoi.shock: log_liq.shift(1) on the build_df_est_raw estimation sample (same regressor & sample as the exceedance / skew tests).

verdicts.NON-NEGLIGIBLE: 90% CI entirely beyond the band on one side (lo90 >= +SESOI or hi90 <= -SESOI).
verdicts.N/A-UNITS: object's units are not a tail probability; SESOI mapping does not apply (z3_winsor).

honest_power_caveat: If MDE@power > SESOI_beta for an object/span, the design is UNDERPOWERED for strict equivalence there: a non-significant result supports only 'we exclude effects larger than MDE', NOT 'the effect is proven negligible'. Recorded per object/span as underpowered_for_equivalence. INCONCLUSIVE + underpowered=True is the canonical 'absence of evidence is not evidence of absence' case.

iqr.underpowered_note: MDE@power <= SESOI_beta (0.00230679 <= 0.0038338): adequately powered to adjudicate equivalence at this span.

p50p95.underpowered_note: MDE@power > SESOI_beta (0.00230679 > 0.00109155): underpowered; can only claim 'exclude effects > 0.00230679', NOT 'proven negligible'.

p10p90.underpowered_note: MDE@power > SESOI_beta (0.00230679 > 0.00141017): underpowered; can only claim 'exclude effects > 0.00230679', NOT 'proven negligible'.
